perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party warrants that it applies reasonable safeguards against the unauthorized disclosure of Proprietary Information and that it will protect such Proprietary Information as least as securely as it protects its own Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party warrants that it applies minimal safeguards against the unauthorized disclosure of Proprietary Information and that it will protect such Proprietary Information with less security than it protects its own Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text weakens the protection standard, stating the Receiving Party uses 'minimal safeguards' and provides 'less security', directly contradicting the original stronger obligation and potentially conflicting with the 'reasonable safeguards' mentioned elsewhere. This leads to an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall be treated and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party for a period of two (2) years from the date of disclosure. The Receiving Party warrants that it applies minimal safeguards against the unauthorized disclosure of Proprietary Information and that it will protect such Proprietary Information with less security than it protects its own Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the protection standard of Proprietary Information by stating the Receiving Party uses 'minimal safeguards' and provides 'less security' than before. This directly contradicts the original requirement of applying 'reasonable safeguards' as described in the original text. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, which establishes the contradiction by mandating lower protection standards than what would typically be considered reasonable, creating an inconsistency regarding the level of security required for Proprietary Information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate two (2) years from the date first written above, except the obligations of confidentiality pursuant to paragraph 3, and the terms of paragraph 4, shall continue for the period specified in paragraph 3.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate one (1) year from the date first written above, except the obligations of confidentiality pursuant to paragraph 3, and the terms of paragraph 4, shall continue for the period specified in paragraph 3.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the termination date of the agreement from two years to one year. This contradicts the confidentiality obligations outlined in paragraph 3, which were originally meant to last two years as stated in the original paragraph 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall be treated and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party for a period of two (2) years from the date of disclosure. The Receiving Party warrants that it applies reasonable safeguards against the unauthorized disclosure of Proprietary Information and that it will protect such Proprietary Information as least as securely as it protects its own Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the termination date of the agreement from two years to one year. The contradicted text states that Proprietary Information shall be treated and safeguarded for a period of two years from the date of disclosure. This creates a contradiction because the agreement now terminates after one year, while the obligation to protect Proprietary Information lasts for two years. This constitutes an in-text contradiction as it introduces conflicting deadlines as defined in Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that it will not disclose the subject matter or terms of this Agreement or the discussions between the Parties without the written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that it will not disclose the subject matter or terms of this Agreement or the discussions between the Parties without the verbal consent of the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'written' to 'verbal' consent directly contradicts the need for a documented agreement on disclosure. This introduces an in-text contradiction because relying on verbal consent creates ambiguity and difficulty in proving consent, undermining the original intention of controlled disclosure and adding uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any amendment to this Agreement must be in writing and signed by authorized officials of each Party. No failure or delay in exercising any right under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'written' with 'verbal' consent for disclosing agreement terms. The contradicted text states that 'Any amendment to this Agreement must be in writing'. This creates a direct contradiction because verbal consent is not in writing, violating the requirement for written amendments and creating an inconsistency, per the explanation provided.
